description: This schematic illustrates the microstructural evolution during annealing of an Fe–8.03Mn–6.10Al–0.4C low-density duplex steel after hot rolling, comparing air-cooled (AC) and furnace-cooled (FC) samples. The upper row depicts the AC path: the initial structure contains δ-ferrite and γ-austenite with α′-martensite; during annealing, part of the martensite reversely transforms to austenite while the remainder degrades into needle-like α-ferrite embedded within austenite bands, refining austenite grains and stabilizing the phase so that the final microstructure consists of δ-ferrite, austenite, and needle-like α-ferrite without martensite after final cooling. The lower row depicts the FC path: the initial structure contains δ-ferrite and austenite; during annealing, austenite enlarges and slight fine equiaxed α-ferrite forms, but the lower stability of coarse austenite causes partial transformation to α′-martensite upon final cooling, yielding δ-ferrite, austenite, equiaxed α-ferrite, and martensite. The figure demonstrates that the prior cooling path controls austenite stability and final annealed phases, thereby governing the strength-ductility synergy.
